metadata.title: Meta / Facebook family - consolidated AI-cluster node (Scale AI, Superintelligence Labs, Hyperion, nuclear, MTIA)
metadata.provenance: Web-verified (Motley Fool, Maginative, Utility Dive, Data Center Frontier, about.fb.com, Tom's Hardware). Consolidates Meta's role previously split across fin-google-amazon-anthropic-meta.json + influence-meta-childsafety.json + energy_web.
cross_refs: influence-meta-childsafety.json (covert age-verification astroturf + litigation - the influence leg)

entities.name: Meta
entities.notes: Capex Q3-2025 $19.37B; FY2025 $70-72B; 2026 guided higher; $30B bond (Oct 2025).

edges.notes: $14.3B for ~49% of Scale AI; Scale CEO Alexandr Wang now leads Meta Superintelligence Labs (MSL). Scale ~$29B valuation. Meta owns ~half the data-labeler that serves the WHOLE AI cluster (incl. Meta's rivals) - a semi-circular, conflicted position that reshaped the data-labeling market.
Meta nuclear deals with Oklo + Vistra + TerraPower for up to 6.6 GW by 2035 (part of the energy leg / SMR thread).
Co-develop 4 generations of MTIA custom AI silicon in 2 years (new chip every <=6 months). Broadcom is one of its 6 XPU customers (Google/Meta/OpenAI/Anthropic +2).

$27B off-balance-sheet JV (Blue Owl 80% / Meta 20%, PIMCO/BlackRock-anchored private credit) for Hyperion, Louisiana - projected total >$200B, 4,000 acres, 10 gas plants 7+GW. The off-balance-sheet template (Enron-SPV lineage).

assessment: Meta is a top-tier AI-core node whose distinctive features are (1) the $14.3B/~49% Scale AI stake (owning the cluster's data-labeler - conflicted/semi-circular), (2) the $27B Blue Owl off-balance-sheet Hyperion SPV (the cleanest 'Enron-lineage' structure in the AI buildout, projected >$200B), (3) a multi-gigawatt nuclear procurement (Oklo/Vistra/TerraPower, 6.6GW) and Broadcom MTIA custom silicon, and (4) the influence leg - covertly funding age-verification astroturf while opposing KOSA (influence-meta-childsafety.json). It sits at the intersection of the AI-core, energy, off-balance-sheet-credit, and identity-control threads.
